source: public web (Google Trends, Reddit, TikTok, news)

trends.title: Satisfying deep-clean transformation videos dominate CleanTok
trends.summary: Long-running 'dirty-to-spotless' transformation clips (ovens, grout, mattreses, cars) filmed in slow satisfying wipes remain one of the most consistently reliable viral formats across CleanTok and Instagram Reels, often set to trending audio with no talking.
trends.angle: P&G Home Care can supply branded 'hero moment' props (Swiffer WetJet reveal shots, Cascade foaming action, Mr. Clean eraser scuff removal) to creators who specialize in this satisfying-transformation format for organic-feeling product placement.
trends.response: Seed a rotating roster of mid-tier CleanTok creators with product bundles (Swiffer, Cascade, Mr. Clean) specifically for slow-pan 'reveal' shots, and repost top UGC to official brand handles with creator credit.

examples.appeal: Hashtag hub for the format — near-wordless before/after reveals with satisfying scrubbing sounds and trending audio drive high completion/watch-through rates.
Reel format favors tight vertical crop on the 'reveal' second, which performs well in IG's Reels recommendation algorithm.
Search hub shows the sub-genre (oven/grout/mattress deep cleans) that consistently racks up saves because viewers bookmark for 'how-to' reference.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Seed authentic, sound-on "hero moment" placements inside existing dirty-to-spotless transformation content to ride CleanTok's satisfying-clean wave — without breaking the ASMR format creators/viewers love.

## Which P&G brand
Primary: Mr. Clean (Magic Eraser scuff removal) and Cascade (foaming action reveal)
Secondary: Swiffer WetJet (floor streak-free reveal), Dawn (grease-cutting close-ups)
Select based on creator's usual dirty surface (oven → Cascade/Degreaser; grout/walls → Mr. Clean; floors → Swiffer)

## Target creators
- CleanTok/#SatisfyingClean creators, 50K–2M followers, no-talking/ASMR style
- Prior transformation videos with organic (non-sponsored) product visibility
- Strong watch-time/completion rate, not just follower count
- Niche crossover ok: car detailing, mattress/upholstery, appliance restoration accounts

## Key message
"The product IS the payoff." Let the foam, scuff-erase, or streak-free reveal be the satisfying climax of the video — brand as hero prop, not spokesperson.

## Hook / opening
Open on the "before" — grime, stain, or gunk in extreme close-up, gross-satisfying tension — then cut straight to slow-motion first contact with product (foaming, wiping, scrubbing). No VO needed; let trending audio carry it.

## Do
- Keep it sound-on, talking-optional, ASMR pacing (slow wipes, crisp foley)
- Show real, unedited transformation (single continuous take preferred)
- Let product label/packaging appear naturally in-frame during the "hero moment"
- Use trending/CapCut-friendly audio already popular in creator's niche
- End on lingering "after" shot (3-5 sec beauty shot of spotless surface)

## Don't
- No scripted testimonials or direct-to-camera brand speak
- Don't over-brand: no logo overlays, lower-thirds, or CTA voiceovers
- Don't fake/exaggerate dirt level — must feel real
- Don't rush the payoff — pacing is the format, don't cut away early
- No competitor comparison call-outs

## Suggested content
- Oven interior: baked-on grease → Cascade foam action → mirror shine
- Bathroom grout/tile scuffs → Mr. Clean Magic Eraser drag shot
- Kitchen floor before/after → Swiffer WetJet streak-free reveal
- Car interior mats/upholstery stain lift → Dawn diluted spray + wipe
- Bonus: split-screen dirty vs. clean side-by-side final shot

## Hashtags & CTA
#CleanTok #SatisfyingClean #DeepClean #CleanWithMe #[BrandName]Clean
CTA (organic, optional caption line): "Which clean was more satisfying? 🧽✨" — drive comments/duets, no hard sell.

## KPI
- View completion rate ≥ 65%
- Engagement rate (likes+comments+shares/views) ≥ 8%
- Sentiment: 90%+ positive/neutral comments
- Earned media value vs. paid spend ratio
- Branded hashtag UGC pickup (duets/stitches) within 14 days
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (native CleanTok feed), cross-post to IG Reels + YouTube Shorts. Vertical, 15-25s, no talking, trending audio, slow-pan macro shots. Silent-scroll-stopper format with on-screen caption text only.

## Caption (ready to post)
the scuff didn't stand a chance 😮‍💨 #MrCleanMagicEraser

no water, no drama, just one swipe and it's gone. tag someone who needs to see this before they repaint their whole wall.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
Extreme close-up on a scuffed-up wall / shoe / baseboard — text overlay: "this stain has been here since 2019"

## Visual direction
- Macro lens, slow steady pan across the dirty surface (crayon marks, shoe scuffs, greasy stove trim)
- Single wipe with Mr. Clean Magic Eraser — capture the literal line where dirty meets clean in real time
- No cuts mid-wipe — that unbroken reveal IS the payoff
- End on a slow zoom-out to the fully clean surface, natural light, satisfying silence beat before audio hits
- Optional: sponge shown getting slightly dirty/foamy for texture/ASMR crumb

## Hashtags
#MrClean #MagicEraser #CleanTok #SatisfyingClean #DeepCleanTransformation #CleaningHacks #ASMRClean #ScuffRemoval

## CTA
Comment "ERASER" and we'll send you a starter pack to film your own before/after 👀

trends.title: Dawn dish soap grease-science hacks keep resurfacing
trends.summary: Creators repeatedly demonstrate Dawn's grease-cutting power in non-dish contexts (degreasing car parts, removing tar/tree sap, wildlife-rescue oil cleanup callbacks) as a 'science hack' that reliably drives comments and shares.
trends.angle: Lean into Dawn's decades-long 'tough on grease, gentle enough for wildlife' equity by amplifying creator demos that show the product outperforming expectations outside the kitchen.
trends.response: Have @dawn official respond/duet the best-performing grease-hack demo videos and push a short-form 'proof' series showing Dawn on engine grime, tar and grill grease to reinforce the wildlife-rescue brand halo.

examples.appeal: Hashtag surfaces the recurring 'Dawn breaks down grease instantly' demo format that performs well because it's a visually satisfying, fast payoff clip.
Search results show the range of off-label grease/oil hacks that spark 'wait, it works on THAT too?' curiosity comments.
Reel format lets creators show a quick before/after swipe test, which is highly shareable and easy to replicate.

briefs.influencer:
# CREATOR/INFLUENCER BRIEF

## Objective
Ride the resurfacing "Dawn grease-science hack" trend to reinforce Dawn's core equity — unmatched grease-cutting power, gentle enough for wildlife — by amplifying authentic creator demos beyond the kitchen sink.

## Which P&G brand
Dawn

## Target creators
- Science/DIY explainer creators (chemistry, "satisfying clean" niches)
- Auto/garage & mechanic creators (grease, tar, sap removal)
- Outdoor/lifestyle & wildlife rescue-adjacent creators (rehab centers, animal sanctuaries — with permission/partnership only)
- Cleaning & life-hack creators with strong demo/before-after formats
- Mid-tier (50K–500K) creators for authenticity; 2-3 macro creators for reach

## Key message
"Dawn's grease-cutting power was born to save wildlife — that's why it works everywhere grease does."

## Hook / opening
"If Dawn is gentle enough to clean an oil-soaked duck, imagine what it does to THIS." [cut to tar/grease/sap demo]

## Do
- Show real, unscripted before-after demos with visible grease/tar/sap
- Reference Dawn's wildlife rescue heritage authentically (International Bird Rescue partnership)
- Use satisfying close-up shots (slow pour, bubble action, wipe-away)
- Include a simple science beat (surfactants breaking down oil molecules) in plain language
- Tag @Dawn and use branded hashtag
- Encourage duets/stitches of existing viral Dawn hacks

## Don't
- Don't stage exaggerated/fake messes or misrepresent product performance
- Don't use real distressed/injured wildlife footage without official partner approval
- Don't apply Dawn to unsafe surfaces (skin irritation risk, engine components with electrical risk) — flag safe-use disclaimer
- Don't disparage competitor products by name
- Don't overclaim medical/industrial-strength claims

## Suggested content
- **TikTok:** 15-30s rapid demo + hook + wildlife callback line, trending audio
- **Instagram Reels:** Before/after carousel + Reel, caption tells the "why" (surfactant science + wildlife legacy)
- **Facebook:** Longer-form (60-90s) storytelling video pairing garage/outdoor demo with a wildlife rescue B-roll clip (licensed) for emotional payoff

## Hashtags & CTA
**Hashtags:** #DawnPowerOfClean #GreaseScience #DawnHack #ToughOnGreaseGentleOnWildlife
**CTA:** "Try the hack, tag @Dawn, show us your toughest grease battle."

## KPI
- Engagement rate ≥6% across platforms
- 3M+ organic video views within 30 days
- 15%+ share/save rate on top-performing assets
- Positive sentiment maintained ≥85% in comments
- Earned media pickups referencing Dawn/wildlife equity
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (15-30s demo video), cross-post to Instagram Reels + Facebook Video. Native creator-style duet/stitch format to ride existing hack videos.

## Caption (ready to post)
Okay but why does this keep working 😳 Dawn's been cutting grease since before "life hacks" were a genre. Same formula that saves oil-covered wildlife also handles your bike chain, your tar-covered tires, and that mystery gunk on the garage floor. One bottle, zero limits. Science, not sorcery. 💙

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"Dawn doesn't just do dishes — it does DAMAGE control."

## Visual direction
Quick-cut montage: hands squeezing Dawn directly onto a greasy bike chain → foam lifting tar off a car bumper → tree sap disappearing off garden gloves. End on the iconic wildlife-rescue photo/clip with text overlay: "Same bottle. Same science. Since day one." Keep it raw/UGC-style lighting, not overly polished — matches trend authenticity.

## Hashtags
#DawnPower #GreaseScience #DawnHack #ToughOnGrease #CleaningHacks #DawnDishSoap #LifeHack

## CTA
"Got a grease disaster Dawn saved? Drop it below — we might feature you 👇"

trends.title: Magic Eraser multi-surface hacks stay a CleanTok staple
trends.summary: Creators continually find new off-label uses for the Magic Eraser — scuffed sneakers, crayon on walls, baseboards, stainless steel — turning it into a recurring 'did you know' hack format across CleanTok and cleaning-hack compilation reels.
trends.angle: Position Mr. Clean as the 'one tool, endless uses' hero by curating and amplifying the best off-label hack demos rather than only showing intended kitchen/bathroom use cases.
trends.response: Launch a UGC-style compilation on the Mr. Clean TikTok/IG showcasing top fan-discovered Magic Eraser hacks (shoes, walls, grout lines) with creator tags, and comment/stitch on trending eraser-hack videos in real time.

examples.appeal: Hashtag hub is full of quick 'one swipe' scuff-removal clips that hook viewers with an immediate visible payoff.
Shoe-whitening use case is especially shareable among younger audiences turning cleaning into a sneaker-care hack.
Reel carousel format lets creators stack multiple surfaces (wall, shoe, sink) in one clip for a multi-payoff hook.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Fuel the existing CleanTok "off-label hack" trend around Magic Eraser by seeding and amplifying creator content that positions it as a "one tool, endless uses" household hero — beyond kitchen/bathroom — driving organic reach, saves/shares, and purchase intent.

## Which P&G brand
Mr. Clean (Magic Eraser)

## Target creators
- Cleaning/organizing creators (CleanTok staples, 10K–500K followers)
- Home hack & "did you know" compilation channels
- Sneakerhead/sneaker care micro-influencers
- Parent/family creators (crayon walls, scuffed floors)
- DIY/renovation and moving-hack creators

## Key message
"One eraser, endless hacks — Magic Eraser cleans way beyond the kitchen sink."

## Hook / opening
"Wait... you can use this on THAT?" — open on an unexpected surface (sneaker, baseboard, wall) before revealing it's Magic Eraser.

## Do
- Show real-time before/after wipe with visible scuff/stain removal
- Use "did you know" or "hack #___" compilation framing
- Test genuinely off-label but safe surfaces (rubber soles, walls, tile grout, patio furniture, stainless appliances)
- Include quick tip: "always test a small area first"
- Keep pacing fast, ASMR-style scrub sounds encouraged
- Tag @MrClean / brand handle and use branded hashtag

## Don't
- Don't use on unfinished wood, glossy/painted surfaces without disclaimer, or anything abrasive-sensitive
- Don't oversell as "magic" fix for everything — keep it credible, surface-tested
- Don't skip the safety/test-first callout
- Don't make it feel like a scripted ad — keep native CleanTok tone

## Suggested content
- "5 Magic Eraser hacks you didn't know" compilation
- Sneaker restoration transformation (dirty → fresh)
- Crayon-on-wall mom hack
- Baseboard/door frame scuff removal montage
- Stainless steel appliance streak-free reveal
- Duet/stitch trending Magic Eraser hack videos with your own surface test

## Hashtags & CTA
#MagicEraserHack #MrClean #CleanTok #OneToolEndlessUses #DidYouKnowClean
CTA: "Tag the surface you want us to Magic Erase next 👇"

## KPI
- Views, saves, shares on hack content (target: outperform brand avg by 2x)
- Engagement rate ≥6%
- UGC hashtag participation volume
- Sentiment maintained ≥85% positive
- Lift in "multi-surface use" association in post-campaign brand tracking
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (multi-clip hack compilation, 15–22s), cross-post to IG Reels + YouTube Shorts. Fast-cut "did you know" format stitched from UGC-style demos.

## Caption (ready to post)
One eraser. Infinite jobs. We didn't even teach it half of these. 🧽✨ Duct-tape residue, scuffed kicks, crayon masterpieces on the wall — the Magic Eraser just says "bet." Drop the wildest thing YOU'VE cleaned with one and we'll feature the best 👇

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"Nobody told me Magic Eraser could do THIS—"

## Visual direction
Rapid-fire split screen / whip-transition compilation:
1. Scuffed white sneaker → spotless in 3 swipes
2. Crayon wall art disappearing in real time
3. Grimy baseboard, half clean/half dirty reveal
4. Stainless steel fridge streaks vanishing
Overlay bold on-screen text per clip ("SNEAKERS?? 👀" / "CRAYON = GONE" / "BASEBOARD GLOW UP"). End on Mr. Clean logo + eraser held up like a trophy. Punchy trending audio, quick zoom-ins on the "reveal" moments.

## Hashtags
#MagicEraser #MrClean #CleanTok #CleaningHacks #OneToolEndlessUses #CleanFluencer #HacksThatWork

## CTA
Comment your craziest Magic Eraser hack — best ones get reposted on our page. Follow for more "wait, it does THAT too?" moments.

trends.title: Laundry-stripping and towel/odor-refresh videos recur seasonally
trends.summary: The 'laundry stripping' trend — soaking towels/sheets to reveal grimy soak water, followed by odor-elimination tips — continues to resurface periodically on CleanTok, often paired with product recommendations for smell rather than just visible dirt.
trends.angle: Use the odor-shock moment of laundry-stripping videos to pivot conversation toward Febreze's between-wash freshness and Cascade's 'clean you can smell' cues, since the trend spikes anxiety about hidden odor/buildup.
trends.response: Have Febreze creators film a complementary 'freshness test' follow-up to popular laundry-stripping videos, and consider light-touch influencer seeding around a sniff-test challenge format.

examples.appeal: The shockingly murky soak-water reveal is a built-in gross-out hook that drives high share/comment rates.
Relatable 'why do my towels smell' framing invites strong comment engagement and duet/response videos.
Before/after soak-water color change is a strong visual hook for Reels thumbnails.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Redirect laundry-stripping anxiety (gross soak water, "hidden grime") toward a proactive, positive solution: odor prevention between washes, not just reactive deep-cleans. Position P&G as the brand that solves the *smell* problem the stripping trend exposes but never fully fixes.

## Which P&G brand
Febreze (lead) — In-Wash Booster + Fabric Refresher spray. Optional pairing: Cascade for "clean you can smell" kitchen/dish angle in crossover content.

## Target creators
- Mid-tier CleanTok creators (50K–500K) known for laundry-stripping/satisfying grime content
- Home & family lifestyle creators (busy parents, pet owners — high odor-anxiety audiences)
- Dorm/apartment-living creators (TikTok college cohort, high towel/sheet odor relevance)
- 1-2 "science-y" clean creators who can credibly explain *why* odor lingers post-wash

## Key message
"Stripping shows you the grime — Febreze handles the smell that comes back before you even see it." Odor isn't just dirt; it's bacteria and buildup regular detergent can miss between washes. Prevention > shock-reveal.

## Hook / opening
"Wait... your towels still smell after ALL THAT stripping?" — open on a post-strip towel, sniff test fails, cut to Febreze fix. Or: "I did the viral laundry-stripping trend and my water was disgusting... but the real problem wasn't visible."

## Do
- Show a real (or reenacted) stripping moment briefly, then pivot fast to the odor-focused solution
- Emphasize the "in-between washes" use case — towels, gym clothes, pet beds
- Use sniff-test / reveal format native to the trend (hands-on, visual, ASMR-adjacent)
- Include one clear product demo (spray, toss in wash, before/after smell claim)
- Keep tone light, mildly humorous — not fear-mongering

## Don't
- Don't disparage the stripping trend itself or call it fake/unnecessary
- Don't overclaim ("kills all bacteria") — stick to odor-elimination language
- Don't make it feel like a straight ad; keep creator's authentic format/editing style
- Don't linger on gross-out visuals longer than needed to make the point

## Suggested content
1. **Stitch/duet format**: React to a popular stripping video, then show your own towels — "clean" by stripping standards but still not fresh — Febreze save.
2. **7-Day Freshness Test**: Use Febreze between washes on towels/gym clothes, diary-style check-ins.
3. **Myth-bust carousel (IG)**: "Stripping removes buildup. Here's what it doesn't remove." Educational, swipeable.
4. **Crossover kitchen bit**: Cascade angle — dishes that look clean but don't smell clean; quick tie-in for FB/IG audiences.

## Hashtags & CTA
#FebrezeFresh #LaundryStripping #CleanTok #SmellTheDifference #FreshBetweenWashes
CTA: "Strip it, then Febreze it — smell the difference between washes."

## KPI
- Engagement rate ≥6% on branded content
- Share/save rate (indicates trend-relevance capture)
- Sentiment split: track positive/neutral shift vs. baseline mixed sentiment
- Attributed lift in Febreze booster/spray search & sales during campaign window
- Earned stitches/duets referencing brand pivot ang
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (short-form video, 15-25s), repurposed as Instagram Reel + Facebook video post

## Caption (ready to post)
That soak water is not it. 😳 But here's the truth: stripping your towels once a month doesn't fix the funk in between washes. That's a Febreze job. Spritz after every use, keep the musty smell out before it moves in. Your towels will thank you (and so will your nose). 🌸

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"You're about to see why your towels smell like that."

## Visual direction
Open on the classic laundry-stripping shot — grimy soak water reveal, quick zoom for shock effect. Cut fast to a split screen: LEFT = towel fresh out of the dryer sitting on a rack for days getting musty. RIGHT = same towel getting a quick Febreze spritz after each use. End on a clean, bright bathroom shelf with Febreze bottle in frame, text overlay: "Between washes > waiting for the ick."

## Hashtags
#Febreze #LaundryStripping #CleanTok #OdorControl #FreshLaundry #TowelTalk #CleanTokTrends #FebrezeFresh

## CTA
Drop a 🫠 if you've seen that soak water before. Then go grab your Febreze — don't wait for stripping day to feel fresh.

trends.title: Dawn's wildlife rescue halo boosts grease-hack praise
trends.summary: Dawn's long-running partnership with wildlife rescue groups (using Dawn to clean oiled birds/animals) keeps resurfacing on social feeds and gets paired with everyday 'Dawn cuts grease' kitchen and Powerwash hack content, reinforcing a strong goodwill/efficacy narrative for the brand.
trends.angle: Lean into the authentic 'Dawn saves wildlife' equity as social proof for grease-cutting power, turning a feel-good rescue story into a performance claim people already trust.
trends.response: Re-share/duet a wildlife-rescue clip on Dawn's TikTok/IG with a caption bridging 'if it's gentle enough to save a duck, it's tough enough on your grease' and pin it alongside a Powerwash grill/oven hack video.

examples.appeal: Emotional animal-rescue footage is inherently high-completion, high-share content that doubles as unscripted brand proof.
Before/after grease-cutting demos are satisfying, short, and easy to duet/stitch, driving strong watch-through rates.
Kitchen 'one bottle, many uses' hacks tap the evergreen cleaning-hack format that consistently outperforms on CleanTok.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Convert the resurfacing "Dawn saves wildlife" goodwill moment into tangible proof of grease-cutting performance—linking heart (rescue) to head (efficacy) so audiences see Dawn as both trusted and effective.

## Which P&G brand
Dawn

## Target creators
- Home-hack & cleaning creators (Powerwash/kitchen hack niche)
- Animal/wildlife rescue accounts & volunteers (authentic partners, not paid actors)
- Lifestyle/parenting creators who post "kitchen SOS" content
- Micro-creators (50K–500K) for trust; 1-2 mid-tier (500K-2M) for reach

## Key message
"The same Dawn that cuts through crude oil on rescued wildlife cuts through grease on your dishes and pans — proven tough, proven gentle."

## Hook / opening
"If Dawn can save an oil-soaked otter... imagine what it does to last night's lasagna pan."

## Do
- Show a real before/after grease-cutting moment (pan, stovetop, Powerwash surface)
- Reference the wildlife rescue legacy briefly and factually (no overclaiming animal welfare outcomes)
- Use warm, authentic tone — feel-good first, performance second
- Include one line connecting "same formula, same power"
- Tag official Dawn account + link to Dawn's wildlife program page if available

## Don't
- Don't use real distressed animal footage unless sourced directly from verified rescue partners with rights/approval
- Don't overstate claims ("kills all bacteria," "saves every animal") — keep it emotionally resonant, not clinical
- Don't make rescue imagery feel exploitative or staged for virality
- Don't bury the grease-cutting demo — it must be visually clear, not just implied

## Suggested content
1. **Split-screen hack**: Wildlife rescue archival clip (licensed) transitions into creator's own greasy-pan-to-sparkling-clean demo
2. **Powerwash + Dawn combo hack**: Creator uses Dawn dish soap in Powerwash dispenser for driveway/patio degreasing, captioned with rescue-inspired hook
3. **Creator testimonial**: "I didn't know Dawn had this whole rescue history until I saw it — now I get why it works so well on grease"
4. **Rescue partner duet/stitch**: Wildlife org shares real footage; home creators stitch with kitchen hack reaction

## Hashtags & CTA
#DawnPower #GreaseGone #DawnSavesWildlife #PowerwashHack
CTA: "Tag someone who needs a Dawn grease miracle — then go check out how Dawn helps rescue wildlife too 🦦"

## KPI
- Engagement rate ≥6% on branded content
- 15M+ combined views across platforms in 30 days
- 20%+ lift in "trustworthy/effective" brand sentiment (social listening)
- 3+ organic pickups/remixes by wildlife rescue orgs or unpaid creators
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (15-20s video), repurposed as Instagram Reel + Facebook video post. Split-screen or fast-cut transition format.

## Caption (ready to post)
If it's gentle enough to save a duck covered in oil, it's strong enough to save your baking sheet from that lasagna incident.

Same Dawn. Same grease-cutting power. Two very different rescues.

This is why we've been doing wildlife cleanup for 45+ years — because if it works on them, it works on literally everything in your kitchen.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"This bird has never met a burnt casserole dish, but Dawn's about to save them both."

## Visual direction
Split screen: Left side = real (or archival-style) footage of a rescued bird/otter being gently cleaned with Dawn, feathers going from oil-slicked to clean. Right side = same Dawn bottle, same squirt motion, cutting through baked-on grease on a sheet pan / Powerwashing a grimy grill grate. Sync the "squeeze bottle" motion as the transition trigger. End on both "subjects" looking shiny/clean side by side — bird + pan. Text overlay: "Wildlife tough. Kitchen tough. Same Dawn."

## Hashtags
#DawnSaves #GreaseHack #DawnDishSoap #PowerWash #CleaningTok #DawnWildlife #KitchenHack #ProofNotPromises

## CTA
Tag someone who needs this reminder that their dish soap is basically a superhero. Then go grab Dawn and see for yourself — link in bio.

trends.title: Swiffer dusters get reinvented for car and blinds
trends.summary: CleanTok continues to churn out creative reuse hacks for Swiffer dusters and WetJet pads (blinds, ceiling fans, car dashboards, baseboards), keeping the brand in constant rotation as a 'life hack' staple rather than just a floor product.
trends.angle: Position Swiffer as a multi-surface hack hero rather than a single-use mop/duster to capture the reuse-hack audience already generating content for free.
trends.response: Seed a branded 'unexpected places to Swiffer' challenge/duet series on TikTok highlighting car interiors, blinds and vents, reposting top creator hacks to Swiffer's own IG Reels.

examples.appeal: Unexpected-use hacks (car dashboard, blinds) surprise viewers and prompt 'I never thought of that' shares.
Quick before/after Reels format is snackable and highly saveable/re-shareable for home organization audiences.
DIY refill/repurpose hacks tap into a thrifty, problem-solving angle that resonates with budget-conscious viewers.

briefs.influencer:
# CREATOR/INFLUENCER BRIEF

## Objective
Ride the existing CleanTok reuse-hack wave to reposition Swiffer Dusters/WetJet pads as a multi-surface "hack hero" — not just a floor tool. Amplify organic momentum (71/99) with seeded content that pushes reuse occasions (car, blinds, fans, baseboards) into mainstream awareness.

## Which P&G brand
Swiffer (hero brand); Febreze as optional secondary tie-in (dusting + light freshness combo content).

## Target creators
- CleanTok/OrganizeTok micro-to-mid creators (10K–500K), high engagement rate over follower count
- Car detailing / car girlies & car dads (auto-care crossover audience)
- Home organization + "cleaning satisfaction" ASMR creators
- Budget/dorm-life & small-space hack creators (college, apartment renters)
- Avoid mega-celebrity talent — this trend thrives on relatable, DIY authenticity

## Key message
"One duster, endless hacks." Swiffer isn't just for floors — it's the multi-surface clean hack your whole space (and car) has been missing.

## Hook / opening
"Wait, you're only using this on your floors?" / "POV: you just found the cheapest car detailing hack in your cleaning closet" / "Things Swiffer never told you it could do..."

## Do
- Show real, unscripted-feeling discovery moments (satisfying dust-grab close-ups)
- Feature at least one *unexpected* surface (car dash/vents, blinds, ceiling fan, baseboards)
- Use trending CleanTok sounds/edit pacing (fast cuts, satisfying reveal)
- Let creators caption in their own voice — hack-tip tone, not ad copy
- Include a soft product mention/label shot, not a hard sell

## Don't
- Don't overscript or make it feel like a commercial
- Don't claim uses outside intended surface safety (no raw engine parts, no liquids near electronics)
- Don't ignore FTC #ad disclosure requirements
- Don't force brand jingles/tagline reads — let hack speak first

## Suggested content
- "5 Places You're Not Using Swiffer (But Should)" carousel/reel
- Car detailing hack duet/stitch trend
- Before/after blinds or ceiling fan reveal with Swiffer glide shot
- "Cleaning closet essentials, reinvented" POV room tour
- Split-screen: floor use vs. surprise reuse

## Hashtags & CTA
**Hashtags:** #SwiffHack #CleanTok #SwiffleReuse #MultiSurfaceHack #SwifferSquad
**CTA:** "Show us YOUR Swiffer hack — duet this and tag @Swiffer for a feature."

## KPI
- Engagement rate ≥ 6% avg across seeded content
- 15+ organic duets/stitches per hero post within 14 days
- Share of voice: Swiffer mentions in reuse-hack content up 20% vs. baseline
- Sentiment maintained ≥ 85% positive
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (15-30s multi-clip montage) → repost to Instagram Reels

## Caption (ready to post)
We invented the duster. Y'all invented the *lifestyle.*

Blinds? Dusted. Dashboard? Dusted. Ceiling fan you've been avoiding since 2022? Also dusted.

Consider this our official co-sign: Swiffer was never just a floor thing. Keep the hacks coming, we're taking notes 📝

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"POV: you bought a Swiffer for the floor and now it runs your whole life"

## Visual direction
Fast-cut montage, handheld/UGC energy:
1. Duster gliding through blinds slat-by-slat (satisfying dust reveal)
2. Swipe across car dashboard + vents
3. Ceiling fan blade wipe from a step stool
4. Baseboards, quick swipe
5. End on a stack of "before" dusty surfaces vs. "after" clean ones, duster head literally grey/dirty (proof it worked)
Text overlays per clip: "the floor duster" → "the blind duster" → "the car duster" → "the fan duster" → "the one duster to rule them all"
Trending audio: upbeat, glow-up/transformation sound

## Hashtags
#SwifferHack #CleanTok #SwifferDuster #LifeHack #CleaningHack #SwifferSquad #DustingHack

## CTA
Duet this with YOUR weirdest Swiffer hack — we'll be stealing... er, *featuring* the best ones 👀

trends.title: Magic Eraser remains CleanTok's go-to miracle hack
trends.summary: Mr. Clean Magic Eraser continues to be one of the most reliably viral 'satisfying clean' products, with creators regularly showing it removing scuffs from shoes, walls, stovetops and appliances in high-satisfaction before/after clips.
trends.angle: Ride the evergreen 'oddly satisfying transformation' format that Magic Eraser content naturally produces to reinforce top-of-mind status in the CleanTok algorithm.
trends.response: Partner with CleanTok creators for a 'scuff to spotless' before/after series and repost the most satisfying transformations on Mr. Clean's TikTok/IG with ASMR-style sound-on captions.

examples.appeal: Extreme before/after scuff removal is a proven satisfying-transformation format that drives high replay and share rates.
Close-up macro shots of grime lifting away trigger the 'oddly satisfying' genre that performs well on Reels.
Sneaker-cleaning niche has a dedicated, highly engaged community that reliably reshares product hacks.

briefs.influencer:
# Mr. Clean Magic Eraser — CleanTok Momentum Brief

## Objective
Capitalize on organic "oddly satisfying transformation" momentum (74/99) to keep Magic Eraser top-of-mind in CleanTok's algorithm and reinforce Mr. Clean as the default answer to "how do I get this off?"

## Which P&G brand
Mr. Clean (Magic Eraser)

## Target creators
- CleanTok / SatisfyingTok creators (10K–500K followers) with proven before/after transformation content
- Home organization & deep-clean niche accounts
- Sneakerheads/shoe-care creators (scuff removal angle)
- Parents/pet-owner lifestyle creators (walls, appliances, scuffed surfaces)
- Micro-creators with high engagement rate > raw follower count

## Key message
One eraser, endless "how did that just happen" moments — Magic Eraser turns everyday scuffs and grime into scroll-stopping satisfaction.

## Hook / opening
Open mid-scrub or on the "before" shot — no intro, no talking for first 2 seconds. Let the contrast do the hooking. Optional text overlay: "wait for it" / "this shouldn't be this satisfying."

## Do
- Show real, unedited before/after (single continuous take preferred)
- Use close-up macro shots on the erasing motion
- Pick high-contrast dirt/scuff surfaces (white shoes, stovetops, walls, appliances)
- Let silence/ASMR scrubbing sound play — minimal voiceover
- End on clean product shot + brand callout in caption, not intrusive on-screen

## Don't
- Don't over-produce with heavy editing/music that kills the ASMR/satisfying feel
- Don't stage exaggerated "too dirty to be real" messes — keep it believable
- Don't over-explain the product; let the visual sell it
- Don't ignore surface safety — no delicate/painted surfaces without disclaimer

## Suggested content
1. "One Wipe Wonders" series — shoes, walls, stove in one video, 3 quick transformations
2. POV cleaning challenge — creator finds worst scuff in their home, live reaction
3. Duet/stitch trend — react to another creator's dirty surface with the eraser save
4. Appliance edition — fridge handles, oven doors, microwave interiors

## Hashtags & CTA
#MagicEraser #CleanTok #OddlySatisfying #MrClean #CleanWithMe
CTA: "Duet this with your dirtiest before/after — let's see what Magic Eraser can fix."

## KPI
- View-through rate on before/after moment (target: 80%+ retention to reveal)
- Engagement rate (saves/shares as proxy for satisfying-content success)
- Branded hashtag volume growth
- Sentiment maintenance ≥ current positive baseline
- Momentum score sustained or increased next tracking cycle
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (vertical video, 15-30s) → repurpose to Instagram Reels + YouTube Shorts
Format: Before/after "satisfying transformation" clip, no music breaks, ASMR-friendly scrubbing sound on

## Caption (ready to post)
CleanTok's favorite plot twist: the scuff was never permanent. It was just waiting for us. 🧽✨ Tag someone who needs to see this before they buy new shoes.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
Text on screen: "POV: your white shoe is about to look brand new" — cut straight to the eraser hitting the scuff.

## Visual direction
- Extreme close-up, one continuous take, no cuts
- Start on the "damage" (scuffed sneaker, marker on wall, or grease-caked stovetop) for 1 sec so the ick is felt
- Wet the Magic Eraser on camera (satisfying squeeze sound)
- Slow, deliberate scrub — let the transformation line move in real time, don't speed it up
- End on a hard freeze frame of the clean result, hold 2 full seconds before caption/logo drop
- Mr. Clean bottle/eraser box cameo in corner, not centered — keep it subtle, let the product do the talking

## Hashtags
#MagicEraser #MrClean #CleanTok #OddlySatisfying #CleaningHack #ThatGirlClean #SatisfyingClean

## CTA
Comment your dirtiest surface — we'll tell you if Magic Eraser can save it. 👇

trends.title: Skeptics question if Febreze truly eliminates odor
trends.summary: A recurring skeptical narrative circulates on social platforms and forums claiming Febreze only masks odors rather than neutralizing them, prompting 'does it actually work' debate threads and reaction videos that mix curiosity with mild criticism.
trends.angle: Directly address the odor-elimination science claim with transparent, demonstrable proof content to defuse recurring skepticism before it hardens into a lasting myth.
trends.response: Publish a short myth-busting explainer video on Febreze's TikTok/IG showing the odor-elimination mechanism (e.g., a controlled smell test on camera) and pin it as a reply to the most common skeptical comments/threads.

examples.appeal: Myth-vs-reality framing invites strong comment debate, which itself drives algorithmic reach.
Reaction-style testing videos are highly relatable and easy for viewers to replicate at home, encouraging duets.
Practical car-freshening hacks give a positive counter-narrative to pair against the skepticism trend.

briefs.influencer:
## Objective
Neutralize the "Febreze just masks odor" skepticism narrative before it calcifies into accepted myth. Replace speculation with visible, third-party-feeling proof that Febreze's Odor Eliminator technology (cyclodextrin molecules trapping odor particles) actually breaks down odor, not just perfumes over it.

## Which P&G brand
Febreze

## Target creators
- Science/chemistry explainers (e.g., "everyday science" TikTokers, 50K–500K followers)
- Cleaning & home skeptics who do "does this product actually work" testing series
- Reddit-native creators/AMA-style hosts with credibility in r/skincareaddiction-style rigor (adapted to r/CleaningTips, r/HomeImprovement)
- Micro-creators with pets, smokers, or gym-bag/odor-heavy lifestyles for authentic before/after use-cases
- Avoid mega-celebrity endorsers — trust here needs to feel earned, not paid-for polish

## Key message
Febreze doesn't cover odors — it eliminates them at a molecular level using cyclodextrin, which physically traps odor molecules so they can't reach your nose. The scent you smell after is a signal the odor is gone, not a mask over it.

## Hook / opening
"I tried to catch Febreze faking it. Here's what happened when I tested it with a black light / smell meter / blind sniff test..."
or
"Everyone says Febreze just masks smells. So I got a chemist to explain what's actually happening in the bottle."

## Do
- Show visible/measurable proof: smell-test panels, blind sniffs, odor-meter apps, before/after fabric swatch tests
- Explain cyclodextrin science simply (use analogy: "a molecular cage that traps stink")
- Test on real skeptics' worst-case odors (gym bags, pet beds, smoke-infused fabric)
- Acknowledge the skepticism directly and respectfully — don't dismiss it
- Encourage duets/stitches of existing skeptical videos to intercept the narrative directly

## Don't
- Don't use overly polished ad-style delivery — keep it raw/UGC-coded
- Don't dodge the "masking" accusation — naming it builds trust
- Don't make unverifiable claims; stick to approved product science
- Don't over-fragrance the demo — let the odor-elimination be the hero, not the scent

## Suggested content
- TikTok: "Chemist reacts to Febreze skeptic videos" duet series
- TikTok/Reddit crossover: blind smell-test challenge with strangers rating fabric samples (some Febreze-treated, some not)
- X thread: simple visual breakdown of cyclodextrin trapping odor molecules (GIF/diagram format)
- Reddit AMA: "I'm a scent chemist, ask me anything about why Febreze isn't just perfume"
- TikTok pet/gym creators: real-life stink-test on worst offenders (cleats, litter box area, car interior)

## Hashtags & CTA
#FebrezeFacts #DoesItActuallyWork #SmellTheDifference #FebrezeScience
CTA: "Try the sniff test yourself — tag us with your before/after."

## KPI
- Shift in sentiment ratio (skeptical vs. neutral/positive mentions) across TikTok/Reddit/X within 30 days
- Engagement rate on science-explainer content vs. brand baseline
- Volume of user-generated "I tested
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok primary (lab-demo video), cross-posted teaser to X, mirrored as a Reddit "proof" post in r/CleaningTips / r/Febreze-adjacent threads with AMA energy.

## Caption (ready to post)
"Masks odor," they said. So we brought a gas chromatograph to prove them wrong. 🧪

Febreze's Odor Clear Technology doesn't perfume your problems — it traps odor molecules and breaks them down at a molecular level. Receipts below. Ask us anything, we're not scared of the smell test.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"You said Febreze just masks odor. So we tested it in an actual lab."

## Visual direction
- Open on a stinky-sock/pet-bed odor sample in a sealed jar (real, gross, relatable).
- Cut to lab scientist (real Febreze chemist if possible) explaining odor molecules on a screen graphic.
- Split screen: molecule count BEFORE spray vs AFTER spray via odor-detection device (visual bar graph dropping, not just "fresh scent" cloud).
- End card: side-by-side jar sniff test with real people (blind test, genuine reactions).
- Text overlay stamps key proof points: "Molecules trapped ✅ Molecules broken down ✅ Not just perfume ✅"

## Hashtags
#FebrezeFacts #OdorScience #DebunkedByData #CleanTok #TrustTheTest #FebrezeChallenge

## CTA
"Still skeptical? Drop your toughest odor test below — we'll run it on camera."

trends.title: Satisfying 'Pink Stuff' Scrub Videos Keep Racking Up Views
trends.summary: The UK-made pink paste cleaner continues to be a staple of oddly-satisfying before/after cleaning content — stovetops, patio furniture, sneakers and grout — driven by ASMR-style transformation reveals rather than any single new campaign.
trends.angle: The format that wins isn't the brand, it's the genre — dramatic before/after grime-removal reveals; Mr. Clean and Dawn can win the format with their own hero products rather than cede it to a UK paste cleaner.
trends.response: Seed a recurring 'before/after' challenge on Mr. Clean's TikTok using Magic Eraser and Mr. Clean liquid on the same grimy surfaces (stovetops, grout, patio) shot in the identical satisfying macro-reveal style, and duet/stitch a few top Pink Stuff videos with a friendly head-to-head.

examples.appeal: Macro close-up before/after wipe reveals trigger the 'oddly satisfying' dopamine hit that drives shares and comments.
Grid of glossy stovetop/pan transformation reels and reshared UGC gives the tag an evergreen, always-fresh feed.
Outdoor patio/deck restorations show dramatic scale-of-mess payoff, ideal for autumn deep-clean content.

briefs.influencer:
# CREATOR/INFLUENCER BRIEF

## Objective
Win the "satisfying scrub" genre back for P&G — not by chasing The Pink Stuff, but by out-performing it at its own format with Mr. Clean and Dawn hero products, driving trial and owned-hashtag volume.

## Which P&G brand
Mr. Clean (Magic Eraser + Mr. Clean liquid) — primary
Dawn (Powerwash + Platinum) — secondary, for kitchen grease reveals

## Target creators
- Mid-tier cleaning/ASMR creators (50K–500K followers) with existing before/after grime content
- Home renovation & "cleantok" niche accounts
- A few oddly-satisfying/ASMR specialists outside cleaning niche (crossover reach)
- Avoid mega-influencers with no cleaning credibility — authenticity > reach here

## Key message
"The most satisfying transformation isn't the paste — it's the eraser." Mr. Clean Magic Eraser and Dawn Powerwash deliver the same jaw-dropping reveal, faster, with tools built for the mess, not just a tub of paste.

## Hook / opening
Open mid-scrub, no context, no logo — grime already half-gone. First 2 seconds must look like every other satisfying video in-feed, then reveal the product as the "twist."
Example line: "Everyone's using pink paste for this... here's what actually happens with one swipe of a Magic Eraser."

## Do
- Lean hard into ASMR sound design (scrub sounds, water rinse, no music overpower)
- Shoot genuinely dirty, real objects — stovetops, sneakers, patio furniture, grout
- Use extreme macro/close-up before-after split or slow pan reveal
- Let the product do the visual talking; minimal voiceover
- Show speed/ease as differentiator (fewer steps, no rinse-repeat)

## Don't
- Don't disparage The Pink Stuff by name — win the format, not a diss track
- Don't overproduce — no studio lighting, no scripts that sound like ads
- Don't bury the product reveal past the first 3 seconds
- Don't use trending audio that's overtly tied to competitor content

## Suggested content
1. "One Wipe" stovetop grease reveal — Mr. Clean Magic Eraser, side-by-side split screen
2. Sneaker sole whitening challenge — before/after slow zoom
3. Patio furniture grime scrub — Dawn Powerwash foam application + rinse reveal
4. Grout line transformation — top-down satisfying pan shot
5. "Duet this" format inviting users to react/recreate with their dirtiest object

## Hashtags & CTA
#MrCleanMagic #OneWipeWonder #DawnPowerwash #SatisfyingScrub #CleanTok
CTA: "Show us your dirtiest before/after — duet with your own Magic Eraser reveal."

## KPI
- Views/engagement rate vs. category benchmark (target: beat avg Pink Stuff video engagement)
- Share of voice within #cleantok satisfying-scrub content (branded vs. unbranded)
- UGC volume: target 500+ organic duets/remixes within 30 days
- Retail sales lift (Magic Eraser, Powerwash) in markets with creator concentration
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok + Instagram Reels primary (9:16, 15-20s), YouTube Shorts as repost. Silent ASMR-style before/after transformation, single continuous scrub shot, no talking head.

## Caption (ready to post)
The genre isn't the flex. The GREASE is the flex.

Mr. Clean Magic Eraser vs. a stovetop that gave up on itself years ago. No music, no cuts, just the sound of grime losing.

You don't need a pink paste when you've got the Eraser.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
Text on screen: "everyone's obsessed with pink paste videos... so we found the crustiest stovetop in America"

## Visual direction
- Static overhead or 3/4 angle shot, good lighting, ASMR mic close on the sponge/eraser contact
- Split-second "before" freeze frame with a zoom-in on the worst grime spot
- Single unbroken wipe reveal — let the transformation do the talking, no jump cuts
- End on a slow pull-back of the fully clean surface with light glare/shine highlight
- On-screen caption ticker: "0% paste. 100% Eraser."
- Consider a grid: 3 clips stitched (stovetop / sneaker sole / patio table) each ending in the same reveal beat

## Hashtags
#MrClean #MagicEraser #CleanTok #SatisfyingClean #BeforeAndAfter #DeepClean #CleaningHacks #ASMRClean

## CTA
Drop your worst grime spot below — we'll pick one to Magic Eraser on camera. 👇

trends.title: Non-Toxic 'Make Your Own Cleaner' Devices Gain Mom Following
trends.summary: Hypochlorous-acid appliance cleaners like Force of Nature keep growing with parents, pet owners and 'chemical-free home' creators who film the electrolyzed-water activation process and tout it as a bleach alternative safe around kids and pets.
trends.angle: The underlying demand is trust and ingredient transparency, not the device itself — P&G Home Care can meet 'clean-label' shoppers with clear ingredient/safety storytelling for existing formulas rather than losing them to a niche appliance brand.
trends.response: Have Dawn/Mr. Clean partner with a couple of trusted 'clean home' and pet-parent creators to publish transparent 'what's actually in this bottle and why it's safe' explainer videos, directly addressing the same safety questions driving people toward hypochlorous-acid brands.

examples.appeal: Watching the little device fizz and turn water into cleaner feels like a science-experiment hook that's inherently video-friendly.
Aesthetic, pastel-toned 'toxin-free home' reels tap into the wellness-mom audience that shares heavily to Stories.
Pet-safe and baby-safe cleaning claims drive strong comment engagement from parents asking follow-up questions.

briefs.influencer:
# CREATOR/INFLUENCER BRIEF

## Objective
Counter the "make-your-own-hypochlorous-cleaner" trend by winning back clean-label-curious parents/pet owners with transparency storytelling — position an existing P&G formula as the trusted, tested, no-DIY-required alternative to bleach.

## Which P&G brand
**Mr. Clean** (Clean Freak / Multi-Surface, EPA List N, bleach-free formulas) — primary
Secondary: **Dawn** (baby-safe dish soap, pet-bath heritage) for pet/baby-adjacent content

## Target creators
- "Chemical-free home" & clean-label moms (50K–500K)
- Pet-parent creators who clean up messes daily
- Micro-creators in crunchy/granola-mom, NICU-mom, or eco-mom niches
- 1–2 credentialed voices (chemist/RN "clean chemistry" explainers) for trust-layer content

## Key message
"You don't need a device to know what's in your cleaner — we already tell you." Ingredient transparency + third-party safety data > DIY electrolysis. Trust is the trend, not the appliance.

## Hook / opening
"I was about to buy a $150 machine to make my own cleaner… until I actually read the label on what's already under my sink."

## Do
- Show full ingredient list on-screen, plain-English translation of each
- Reference EPA/ASPCA-safe claims, pediatrician/vet-approved language where accurate
- Film real mess + real clean (kitchen counter, high chair, pet area)
- Address the "is bleach bad?" question directly and calmly — myth vs. fact tone
- Link to brand ingredient transparency page/QR in caption

## Don't
- Don't disparage Force of Nature by name or mock the device community
- Don't claim "chemical-free" (everything is a chemical) — say "safe, tested, transparent" instead
- Don't make unverified medical/safety claims — stick to approved label language
- Don't over-produce; keep it kitchen-table authentic, not ad-like

## Suggested content
1. **"Label Read" video**: creator picks up product, reads/translates ingredients live, compares safety data to DIY device claims
2. **Side-by-side clean test**: DIY hypochlorous spray vs. Mr. Clean on same mess, same time, note effort/cost/consistency
3. **"Ask a chemist" duet/stitch**: credentialed creator responds to trending FoN video with calm, factual breakdown
4. **Pet/baby safety myth-bust**: "Is this safe if my dog licks it?" walkthrough using brand's own safety FAQ

## Hashtags & CTA
`#CleanLabelHome` `#IngredientTransparency` `#ChemistryNotChemicals` `#MrCleanSafe` `#PGHomeCare`
CTA: "Check what's really in your cleaner — link in bio" → drives to ingredient transparency hub

## KPI
- Engagement rate ≥6% on trend-jacking content
- Sentiment: ≥70% positive/neutral comment sentiment on "safety" mentions
- Click-through to ingredient page ≥3%
- Share of voice vs. Force of Nature mentions in comments (track brand comparisons)
- Track lift in "safe for kids/pets" association via social listening (baseline vs. +30 days)
briefs.post:
## Platform & format
TikTok + Instagram Reels (vertical video, 15–20s), repurposed as an Instagram/Facebook carousel with an ingredient-label graphic as slide 2.

## Caption (ready to post)
Not everything "chemical-free" needs a science kit on your counter. 🧪❌

We love that clean-label shopping is having a moment — it means you're asking the right questions. So here's the honest answer: Mr. Clean formulas are tested for safety, tough on grime, and made with ingredients we're happy to show you. No electrolysis required. No guessing what "activated" means. Just a label you can actually read, on a bottle that's ready when you are.

Transparency shouldn't be a DIY project. It should be on every label already in your cart.

## Hook (first line / first 2s)
"You don't need a machine to know what's in your cleaner."

## Visual direction
Split-screen style: left side shows the DIY device trend in fast-motion (water, salt, blinking light — playful, not mocking); right side shows a hand grabbing Mr. Clean, flipping the bottle to the ingredient/safety info, wiping down a high-chair tray or dog bowl area in one smooth swipe. End on text overlay: "Same peace of mind. Zero assembly." Warm, kitchen-lit, real-home aesthetic — no studio gloss.

## Hashtags
#MrClean #CleanLabel #IngredientTransparency #NonToxicHome #PetSafeCleaning #ChemicalFreeHome #CleanWithConfidence

## CTA
Tap the link in bio to see exactly what's inside every Mr. Clean formula — because you shouldn't have to build trust from a kit.

trends.title: 'Smells Like Fabuloso' Mop Routine Videos Stay Evergreen
trends.summary: Fabuloso's signature scent and bright purple/pink color keep fueling floor-mopping ritual and satisfying-suds TikToks, with creators layering it into full 'clean girl' home-reset routines alongside other purple/pink multi-surface cleaners.
trends.angle: Scent and color are doing the emotional/aesthetic work in these videos more than cleaning performance, which is a lane Swiffer and Mr. Clean can enter with their own bold scent lines and colorful packaging in routine-reset content.
trends.response: Brief a batch of home-organization/'clean girl' creators to feature Swiffer WetJet or Mr. Clean scented lines inside full house-reset routine videos, matching the same mop-and-mood aesthetic that makes the Fabuloso clips shareable.

examples.appeal: Slow-motion sudsy mop shots paired with satisfying scent commentary make for calming, sensory ASMR-style viewing.
Full 'clean with me' home-reset reels give the product a lifestyle halo beyond just functional cleaning.
Trend-stitched routine videos with upbeat audio invite duets and remixes, extending reach organically.

briefs.influencer:
# CREATOR/INFLUENCER BRIEF

## Objective
Ride the "Smells Like Fabuloso" mop-routine wave by inserting Mr. Clean and Swiffer into the "clean girl" home-reset aesthetic — winning on scent + color, not just performance claims — before the trend cools.

## Which P&G brand
**Mr. Clean** (bold scent lines, iconic bottle color/branding for mop-bucket ritual content) + **Swiffer** (colorful WetJet/Sweeper pads for satisfying multi-surface reset shots). Lead with Mr. Clean for the mop-specific format; Swiffer for full room-reset sequences.

- Avoid pure deep-clean/DIY channels — this is aesthetic-led, not tutorial-led

## Hashtags & CTA
`#CleanReset #MrClean #Swiffer #CleanTok #SmellsLikeClean #HomeReset`
CTA: "Tag your signature scent" / "Duet your reset routine with your go-to cleaner"

## KPI
- Views/completion rate on branded mop-routine videos (target: match or beat organic Fabu

TikTok + IG Reels — POV mop routine video, trending audio, satisfying suds close-ups, quick-cut "clean girl reset" edit.

Open on the Mr. Clean bottle glowing under warm kitchen light, pour shot in slow-mo with bubbles blooming in the bucket. trends.title: Refillable Tablet Cleaners Ride Sustainable-Home Aesthetic Wave
trends.summary: Reusable-bottle refill tablets from brands like Blueland and Method continue to gain traction in minimalist, sustainability-focused home content, prized for reducing plastic waste and looking good on open shelving.
trends.angle: The appeal here is packaging aesthetics and waste-reduction values, not superior cleaning power, giving P&G Home Care room to test refill/concentrate formats under existing trusted brand names to capture the sustainability-minded shopper.
trends.response: Pilot a limited-run refillable/concentrate SKU (e.g., Dawn or Mr. Clean concentrate pods) and seed it with sustainable-home organizer creators shooting the same clean-shelf, glass-bottle aesthetic used by Blueland/Method content.

examples.appeal: Minimalist glass-bottle shelf styling photographs beautifully, fitting the broader 'aesthetic pantry' content wave.
Watching a tablet dissolve into a spray bottle is a quick, satisfying transformation clip that's easy to loop.
Bright bottle colors and plant-based claims resonate with eco-conscious viewers who comment asking where to buy.
